-- 1 of 4 --

Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 6
!
Next is warming your list.
!
We've covered quite a bit when we talk about the shot across the bow. This is huge for
warming the list. You saw how some e-mails I sent were all about being likable, they
give content. Already a lot of that going is on, this isn't that complicated.
!
If you want to warm your list, you give them good free stuff. That always
works.
!
No matter how cold the list generally is (and trust me everyone does it at one time or
another and probably repeatedly), you send them two or three pieces of good content.
!
The other thing you do is come back and give them some reason why, and say "Hey I'm
sorry, whatever the reason... I forgot my logins credentials" obviously I'm joking around
there.
!
Just say "Hey I got busy, I had some personal things come up" whatever it is, give
them a reason why and then say, I'm really sorry for not having sent you anything but
I've got some really awesome for you. Go take a look at this special report or this
special video, give them some content.
!
Don't be afraid to be personal, especially in that apology, if you give them the reason
you've let the list go cold it will make a difference. Coming up on prelaunch, it's often
good to give a little bit of extra content and down here I've got to be aspirational. Even
if you just tell them a story of someone who achieved something in market or nature, if
it's playing guitar, or carving wooden dolls or growing tomatoes.
!
You can tell a story of how someone succeeded, especially if they succeeded after they
struggled. People love to have someone to cheer on, someone that went through some
struggles, that's very easy to be aspirational.
!
We got the shot across the bow, its wonderful for creating prelaunch warmth right
before the prelaunch.
!
Now let's talk about your launch. Let's take a look at current assets.
!
Earlier in this course, we went through sort of an asset inventory for your entire launch.
!
Now, we're going to look at current assets as they relate to pre-prelaunch.
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 4 --

Things you can think about that you might be able to exploit in your pre-prelaunch to
get things cranking, to get that little buzz going, to warm the relationship with your list,
to gather data and your offer or to get a data and objections or gather data in possible
launch story. Everything we talked about in the prelaunch. These are potential assets
that could help you.
!
You have your prospect list, your client or customer list, maybe you don't have list
yet but if you do those are incredibly powerful for your pre-prelaunch. You may have
raving fans, they could be fans for your products or they could be fans for you. Lot of
times, when you're starting out it's easier to have people who are fans of you, rather
than your products. Especially if you haven't put out any products yet.
!
You might have a blog, you could have JV's and affiliates, you saw how I used my
JV's and got them all excited.
!
Social media presence, Youtube, Facebook, Twitter any others that can be really
powerful for creating the initial buzz. I've often used Twitter and Facebook to drop little
hands about things that are coming.
!
Forums, preferably forums we have a presence, you don't want to just all the sudden
go into a forum that no one knows you and start posting stuff about your business.
That's spamming the forum, you don't want to do that. We're talking about forums
where you have a presence.
!
Relationships with mavens and connectors and persons of influence.
!
Relationships with top blogs, this is where you could possibly be a guest blogger at a
blog, or even if it's a blog where you frequently make comments and you're known on
that blog. Speaking at events, if you can get on stage in an event it can possibly tie
in to your prelaunch.
!
Teleseminars or a webinars, or other places where you might get interviewed.
!
These are some of the assets that you might have, that could be exploited in a
prelaunch.
!
Let's go to the objectives now.
!
First of all, you may have to go back and review what's your launch objective.
!
What do you want from your launch?
!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 4 --

Before you can figure out what your pre-prelaunch objective is, you have to figure out
what the bigger picture is.
!
I think it's really important to get the bigger picture and start to drill down when you're
involved in one of this launches. Pre-prelaunch objective could be: identify the offer,
identify objections, identify the launch story, could be gather testimonial. You saw how I
did that with the testimonial cruncher.
!
Build buzz, warm the relationship with your list, get JV and affiliates excited to promote.
With almost any pre-prelaunch you definitely want to build buzz, and you want to warm
the relationship with your list. Those are probably the two most important.
!
After identifying your offer, the objection is the real key. Often, when you identify the
offer, you'll surface those objections.
!
You'll find out what the objections are, probably just going through this process a lot of
times you do that. Other things to think about when you're looking at your pre-
prelaunch objective is, who do you want talking about your launch? Hopefully it's the
person of influence, the people that can drive you some traffic, and people that will be
open to your launch and will be excited. Usually those connectors, those mavens, and
the persons of influence.
!
Finally who do you want buying your product, and how do you reach them, where do
they hang out? If you're going to generate some pre-prelaunch buzz you don't want a
bunch of people that will never buy your product that aren’t in your target market.
!
These are just things to think about as we start to put together the pre-prelaunch plan.
!
As I mentioned in the intro, this is really powerful stuff. People outside of Product
Launch Formula don't even know this stuff exist. They think they can look at a launch
and reverse engineer what's happening. But they completely miss big, huge chunks of
the process including the pre-prelaunch. This is incredibly powerful, it's how you get the
story out there, and get the people interested in your story, interested in the launch
story, interested in the launch, before he even go live with your prelaunch.
You have them anticipating your prelaunch, you're getting them excited before their BS
detector goes into the red zone, before they even feel like they're getting pitched.
!
The shot across the bow is super, super powerful. It's a great way of letting people
know something is coming, and get the anticipation building, a great way to build early
buzz to warm your list and also help you identify the offer, and the objection in the
story. Really, really powerful stuff and you want to put this to use for you in your
launch.
